news.title: Maharashtra keeps ready reckoner rates unchanged for FY27
news.para: Maharashtra will keep ready reckoner rates unchanged for the financial year 2026-27. This decision aims to ease the financial burden on citizens during property transactions. Real estate sector stability is expected. Stamp duty and registration revenue targets are set for the upcoming fiscal year. The I-Sarita digital system is a significant contributor to collections.

news.title: Mindspace REIT acquires 2.6 million sq ft office asset in Chennai for ₹2,541 crore
news.para: Mindspace Business Parks REIT has bought a large office property in Chennai for approximately ₹2,541 crore. This acquisition significantly boosts its presence in South India. The deal includes existing office space and new development expected by March 2027. This move increases Chennai's share in Mindspace REIT's overall portfolio. The transaction was completed under a special arrangement with its sponsor.

news.title: IBBI suspends insolvency professional's registration for two years in Supertech case
news.para: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) has suspended insolvency professional Hitesh Goel for two years due to multiple lapses in handling Supertech Ltd and Nobility Estates insolvency proceedings. Goel failed to disclose key details in the Information Memorandum and delayed crucial meetings and filings, impacting thousands of homebuyers.

news.title: Oberoi Realty to redevelop 1,740 sq meters land in Mumbai
news.para: Oberoi Realty is set to redevelop a prime ~1,740 sq meter plot in Mumbai's Malabar Hill, aiming for a significant 51,000 sq ft free sale component. This follows a similar cluster development initiative on Peddar Road, where the company secured 1.40 lakh sq ft. These strategic moves highlight Oberoi Realty's expansion in premium Mumbai locales.

news.title: JPMorgan must face Wells Fargo lawsuit over troubled $481 million real estate loan
news.para: A federal judge has rejected JPMorgan Chase's attempt to dismiss a lawsuit filed by Wells Fargo. Wells Fargo is seeking to recover losses for investors in a defaulted commercial real estate loan. The judge found that Wells Fargo adequately alleged JPMorgan knew of a default event by Chetrit Group. Chetrit Group had taken out a large loan in 2019.

news.title: Cement volumes to grow 7-8% in FY27; profitability to face pressure: ICRA
news.para: In FY27, India's cement sector anticipates a robust growth trajectory of 7-8%, fueled primarily by the boom in housing and infrastructure development. Yet, the rising tides of fuel and freight expenses pose a financial challenge, likely impacting operating profits per tonne. Companies may attempt to adjust pricing strategies, though significant increases might be surprising.

news.title: Revolutionizing real estate sector leveraging technology
news.para: For real estate businesses aiming for the next level, leveraging technology is crucial. Digital transformation fuels improved collaboration and efficient automation, streamlining operations and boosting operational transparency. Highbar Technocrat specializes in SAP solutions designed specifically for real estate and infrastructure, paving the way for organizations to transform into intelligent enterprises.

news.title: From April 1, Gurugram residents must pay 18% interest on unpaid property tax, water bill
news.para: Gurugram residents have a deadline of Tuesday to settle outstanding property tax and water bills. After this date, unpaid property tax will attract 18% annual interest, and overdue water bills will face a surcharge. MCG offices will remain open on Tuesday to facilitate these payments. Timely collection aids city development. Residents can pay online or offline.

news.title: Enzyme Office Spaces signs 3.5 lakh sq ft in Bengaluru, expands footprint
news.para: Enzyme Office Spaces has secured 3.5 lakh sq ft in Bengaluru, expanding its presence in Whitefield, HSR Layout, and Hebbal. These new managed office spaces, offering 5,000 seats, are projected to generate ₹50 crore in annual revenue. The company plans further expansion, driven by demand for flexible workspaces.

news.title: Alok Aggarwal retires, Brookfield India REIT appoints Shashank Jain as new CEO
news.para: Brookfield India REIT's manager has appointed Shashank Jain as its new Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director. Jain will take over from Alok Aggarwal on July 1, 2026. Aggarwal is retiring after leading the REIT since its beginning. Charu Thapar and Gaurav Bhatia are also named key personnel. The REIT board approved issuing commercial papers worth up to ₹1,000 crore.

news.title: Blackstone sells Fidere residential portfolio in Spain to Brookfield for $1.4 billion
news.para: Blackstone has divested its entire Fidere residential portfolio in Spain to Brookfield Asset Management for a substantial 1.2 billion euros. This significant transaction, valued at a net 1.05 billion euros, marks the largest multifamily deal in Spain since the 2007-2009 financial crisis. The portfolio comprises approximately 5,000 housing units spread across 47 buildings in Madrid.

news.title: Premium hotels pay UD tax after Jaipur civic body seals properties
news.para: Jaipur Municipal Corporation sealed properties of Hotel Marriott and Hotel Ramada for outstanding urban development tax. Following the action, both hotel managements cleared their dues, amounting to Rs 5 crore and Rs 1 crore respectively, leading to the unsealing of their properties within two hours.

news.title: Malaika Arora Khan lease out apartment in Bandra West, Mumbai for ₹1.17 crore
news.para: Malaika Arora Khan has secured a new apartment in Bandra West, Mumbai, with a substantial monthly rent starting at ₹3.10 lakh. The 36-month lease agreement, registered in March 2026, includes a significant ₹20 lakh security deposit.  Rent will see a 5% annual increase, culminating in a total rental value of ₹1.17 crore over three years.

news.title: In a first, Ranchi civic body collects ₹108 crore in taxes
news.para: Ranchi Municipal Corporation has achieved a record Rs 108 crore in tax collection this financial year, with property tax contributing a significant Rs 100 crore. This surge, attributed to intensified drives and digital payment systems, will fund civic infrastructure improvements. Traders note the convenience of online payment systems has boosted compliance.

news.title: Belagavi civic body revises property tax rates starting April 1
news.para: Belagavi City Corporation has revised property tax rates, increasing them by 3% for residential properties and 4% for commercial, industrial, and plots, effective April 1. This move aims to boost annual revenue by Rs 2-3 crore, with a 5% discount offered for early payment.

news.title: Download allotment letters from Lucknow development body's website
news.para: Lucknow Development Authority now requires allottees to download allotment letters from its website within three months to streamline the process and enhance transparency. Failure to do so will result in automatic cancellation without notice, with registration amounts refunded after deductions. This move aims to reduce delays and prevent fraudulent practices.

news.title: Ahmedabad development body's new draft development plan for 2041 likely in June
news.para: The Ahmedabad Urban Development Authority's (Auda) long-awaited city development plan is set for public release in June, focusing on a 20-year vision up to 2041 and 2047. This ambitious plan aims to significantly upgrade city infrastructure, incorporating new areas and potentially relocating industries to reduce air pollution ahead of the 2030 Commonwealth Games and a bid for the 2036 Olympics.

news.title: Delhi: NDMC tax reform may cut property tax up to 50%
news.para: The New Delhi Municipal Council plans to implement a Unit Area Method for property tax. This new system could slash taxes by 30 to 50 percent, especially for older, self-occupied properties. It aims for fairer taxation by assessing properties based on unit area rather than rental value. Traders welcome the move but seek clarity on tax rates and enforcement.

news.title: Fire safety must stay in building code: BIS panel
news.para: A technical committee of the Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) has opposed the deregulation cell's proposal to remove fire safety provisions from the National Building Code, citing recent fatal fires. While accepting some recommendations, the committee insists fire safety must remain in the code, not as a separate handbook, emphasizing its crucial role in preventing death traps.
